focus: SF1 predecessor reviews, GX7 competitor data, driver trust issues, mid-high handicap driver struggles

PAIN.quote: I don't even use one. I tee off with my old 5 wood which, for whatever reason, is the most consistent club in my bag. It's an almost automatic 220-240 right down the pipe. But for some reason, whenever I attempt to tee off with any of the cheap drivers that have accumulated in my parents shed, I fail miserably
PAIN.source: GolfWRX - Mid-High Capper Needs Driver Advice
PAIN.context: Can hit 5 wood but fails with driver

PAIN.quote: Could my issue be that I don't know how to swing a driver properly, as opposed to the driver itself? I'm assuming that with a guy who would be Masters level fist pumping after breaking 90, that this could very well be my problem
PAIN.source: GolfWRX - Mid-High Capper Needs Driver Advice

PAIN.quote: My short game is excellent but i just can't trust my Driver
PAIN.source: TheSandTrap - Always Almost There But I Can't Trust It
PAIN.context: Can't trust driver despite good short game

PAIN.quote: I always feel like I could lose it at any moment. Is it something that anyone else has experienced?
PAIN.source: TheSandTrap - Always Almost There But I Can't Trust It
PAIN.context: Constant fear of losing driver swing

PAIN.quote: When I was on the course I really felt like I was on the verge of losing it the entire time. I always felt like I would start blocking them out or my classic snap hook at any time
PAIN.source: TheSandTrap - Always Almost There But I Can't Trust It
PAIN.context: On verge of losing it every shot

PAIN.quote: My rounds depend on how I drive. Good driving = confidence which in turn means good scores. If I'm driving poorly I lose confidence and don't shoot as well. There's typically a 8-10 stroke difference between the two
PAIN.source: TheSandTrap - Always Almost There But I Can't Trust It

PAIN.quote: Got paired with a random who had that driver, I can confirm, he was still able to slice it

I purchased a group of three Gx-7's. 14,18 and 21. I am a 20 hc. None of these three measure up to my existing conventional clubs(Callaway). The driver (14) is 20 yds shorter and dispersion is slightly less due to the shorter shots. These clubs are not worth buying

HOPE.quote: From a distance standpoint, the SF1 hung with my PING. The longest drives with the SF1 were as long as all but my two longest with the PING... On average, the SF1 was only eight yards behind my gamer
HOPE.context: SF1 competitive on distance vs PING G425 Max

HOPE.quote: Don't underestimate the distance this SF1 driver is capable of. If you can be 30 or so yards closer to the center and only a few yards shorter, you could save a lot of strokes
HOPE.context: 30 yards closer, few yards shorter = strokes saved

HOPE.quote: I purchased the GX-7 in April. It performed exactly as they said it would. I get at least 10 more yards off the tee with an 85 mph swing speed. In ten years of playing golf, I've never been able to hit a 3 or 4 wood off the deck so I used my five wood. The GX-7 gives me a consistent 180 yards off the deck
G25. The look inspires confidence and it is just a machine. Great combo of forgiveness and distance. To date, it is the best driver I have owned
HOPE.source: GolfWRX - Mid-High Capper Needs Driver Advice

HOPE.quote: I am 65 years old and I hit the ball pretty far. I just purchased the GX7 and got rid of my Ping G410. I am hitting the GX-7 not as long as the Ping but way more accurately (about 4 plus) in the fairway. Heck, I would have hit a seven iron off the tee to be in the fairway
I cranked out multiple 280-yard-plus drives with the SF1, a good poke regardless of the driver in hand by my standards
HOPE.context: SF1 can still produce long drives

ROOT_CAUSE.quote: I think it is just a mental thing we fall into. Granted I just broke 90 and then shot a 99 the next round. I really cant trust my driver all that much either
ROOT_CAUSE.source: TheSandTrap - Always Almost There But I Can't Trust It

ROOT_CAUSE.quote: Do you know what causes you to snap-hook or block in your driver swing once in a while? Perhaps once you know why the problems happen you won't have any apprehension about the swing faults
ROOT_CAUSE.source: TheSandTrap - Always Almost There But I Can't Trust It

ROOT_CAUSE.quote: More of my flexibility is returning and I'm over swinging the clubs. I've always done that and after 12 years the body wouldn't let me. Cut my back swing to way under swinging, and brought back a lot of the accuracy I had
ROOT_CAUSE.source: TheSandTrap - Always Almost There But I Can't Trust It

ROOT_CAUSE.quote: It is still possible to hit a slice with the SF1 driver. If you visit the product page and believe every word there, you might be tempted to think you've hit your last slice ever, but don't get too excited

I don't step up to a tee box hoping not to slice. So when I put a normal swing on the ball with a driver designed primarily to correct a slice, it should come as no surprise that I saw the ball fly so far in the other direction

SOLUTIONS_TRIED.quote: I would just get working on your driver to build confidence and a consistent approach
SOLUTIONS_TRIED.source: TheSandTrap - Always Almost There But I Can't Trust It

SOLUTIONS_TRIED.quote: Take a video (down the line) of a dozen driver swings, forgetting the camera is there, and try to capture the snap-hook or block when they happen without thinking about them
SOLUTIONS_TRIED.source: TheSandTrap - Always Almost There But I Can't Trust It

SOLUTIONS_TRIED.quote: Whatever you do, take the hint from the clubs you can currently hit and not hit... don't get a long driver. Like you said; 44 inches is probably max for you
SOLUTIONS_TRIED.source: GolfWRX - Mid-High Capper Needs Driver Advice

SOLUTIONS_TRIED.quote: Not that getting fit isn't a good thing, but I'd just go buy this for the princely sum of $20. That's going to sort you out a heckuva lot better than just dropping money on a driver
SOLUTIONS_TRIED.source: GolfWRX - Mid-High Capper Needs Driver Advice
SOLUTIONS_TRIED.context: Cheap used club better than expensive new

SOLUTIONS_TRIED.quote: Maybe a lesson or two that specifically looks at my driver/wood play would be more helpful than some shiny new tech
SOLUTIONS_TRIED.source: GolfWRX - Mid-High Capper Needs Driver Advice

COMPETITOR_MECHANISM.quote: The GX-7 was shown to be far from terrible. However, the data does not completely support the claims made in the promotional video. The results aren't likely to motivate you to lock your driver in the closet
COMPETITOR_MECHANISM.context: GX7 decent but doesn't match claims

COMPETITOR_MECHANISM.quote: The driver achieved, on average the highest ball speeds and longest carries. While the GX-7 produced significantly shorter in distance than the driver, it finished just under four yards closer to the target line on average
COMPETITOR_MECHANISM.context: GX7 shorter but 4 yards more accurate

COMPETITOR_MECHANISM.quote: Those struggling to hit it straight (and I mean really struggling) – perhaps those with handicaps above 20 – could benefit from the decreased distance and increased accuracy

The crown of the SF1 driver is what Performance Golf calls an AeroSquare crown. I can't tell you whether or not that design really helps increase toe speed during the downswing, as PG claims, but visually, if you're struggling with a slice, it's confidence-inspiring

If a slice is not your primary shot shape, I think there's a better chance this driver will hurt you than help you. Anybody can hit a slice from time to time, but if you have a two-way miss or a fairly neutral ball flight for the most part, the SF1 driver could cause more harm than good

VILLAIN.quote: Golf infomercials or AS SEEN ON TV golf products were big business in the 90's and early 2000's. The Alien Wedge, Medicus, Orlimar, F2 Wedges, The Haney Blueprint, Tight Lies, Perfect Club, Jack 'The Hammer' Hamm, Liquidmetal, Adams Golf... all seemed to be laughing at us at 2 AM all the way to the bank

You should test all these gimmicks. This is the biggest piece of s**t I've seen. Maybe once exposed we won't have to see these stupid commercials. Killing infomercial super clubs is far more important than comparing major brands
Physics is whispering in my ear and saying, Um, no

That joke of a product and company Alien Golf, sold over $200,000,000 worth of wedges in just over 3 years. To put that in perspective, that's significantly more in US sales than some major equipment manufacturers did in all of 2016

If you're having trouble hitting your clubs well, no miracle club is going to help you; you'll still continue to apply your bad habits. Your money would be better spent getting some lessons, instead of investing in these gimmicks
VILLAIN.context: Miracle clubs don't fix bad habits

sources_covered: TheSandTrap - Always Almost There But I Can't Trust It
GolfWRX - Mid-High Capper Needs Driver Advice
high_value_themes_identified: SF1 only 8 yards behind PING G425 Max
GX7 decent but doesn't match claims
Can't trust driver despite good short game
8-10 stroke swing based on driver confidence
History of infomercial scams ($200M Alien Wedge)
